title: Will Ethereum reach $2,400 April 13-19?

case.title: Will Ethereum reach $2,400 April 13-19?
case.description: What price will Ethereum hit April 13-19?

initial_forecast.question: Will Ethereum reach $2,400 April 13-19?
initial_forecast.time_horizon_label: Apr 13-19 monitoring window through venue-specific threshold resolution
initial_forecast.rationale_summary: ETH reaching $2,400 during Apr 13-19 remains more likely than not because the level is nearby and the mechanics appear touch-friendly, but the market's 76% price is still slightly rich relative to the bounded evidence, so the disciplined posture is watch-only rather than chasing Yes.

latest_forecast.question: Will Ethereum reach $2,400 April 13-19?
latest_forecast.rationale_summary: Ethereum very likely already satisfied this Binance-specific $2,400 touch contract because bundled evidence showed an in-window high above the threshold, but the residual uncertainty is narrow settlement-surface and archival-proof risk, so the disciplined output remains watch-only despite fair value above market.

forecast_rows.question: Will Ethereum reach $2,400 April 13-19?
forecast_rows.time_horizon_label: Apr 13-19 Binance ETH/USDT intrawindow high-touch resolution
forecast_rows.rationale_summary: ETH is likely to hit $2,400 during Apr 13-19 because Binance touch mechanics and the near-miss around 2395 make Yes genuinely favored, but the market's 90.5% price still overstates how automatic that last few dollars really are, so the disciplined posture is watch-only rather than chasing Yes.

forecast_rows.question: Will Ethereum reach $2,400 April 13-19?
forecast_rows.time_horizon_label: Apr 13-19 Binance ETH/USDT intrawindow high-touch resolution
forecast_rows.rationale_summary: ETH is still more likely than not to hit $2,400 during Apr 13-19 because a Binance touch-style trigger with spot already within about 0.5% of the threshold is favorable to Yes, but the market's 92.35% price remains too aggressive for a threshold that had not yet been verified as hit on the governing source during reviewed checks, so the disciplined posture is watch-only rather than chasing Yes.

forecast_rows.question: Will Ethereum reach $2,400 April 13-19?
forecast_rows.rationale_summary: Ethereum very likely already satisfied this Binance-specific $2,400 touch contract because bundled evidence showed an in-window high above the threshold, but the residual uncertainty is narrow settlement-surface and archival-proof risk, so the disciplined output remains watch-only despite fair value above market.

key_assumptions: Polymarket's API-visible rule text matches the actual resolving contract.
Binance's reported 24-hour high above 2400 corresponds to a qualifying minute within the Apr 13-19 ET window.
strongest_supports: Polymarket metadata explicitly defines the source of truth as Binance ETH/USDT 1-minute candle High and showed the market resolved Yes.
Binance public ticker reported ETHUSDT highPrice 2415.50, above the 2400 threshold.
strongest_disconfirmers: The extra verification used Binance's 24-hour summary ticker rather than the exact 1-minute candle table named in the rules.
persona_runs.timing_relevance: High: this is a narrow weekly window and the resolution depends on whether the threshold was hit during Apr 13-19 ET.
persona_runs.source_quality_view: High-quality primary rules/state source plus high-quality same-venue verification; independence medium and source-of-truth ambiguity low after API check.

key_assumptions: The embedded Polymarket rules text extracted from page source is operative for this outcome.
The resolved/closed state shown in page source is current and not a stale frontend artifact.
Later sub-$2,400 trading does not matter once a qualifying Binance 1-minute high already occurred.
strongest_supports: Polymarket embedded rules specify Binance ETH/USDT 1-minute Highs as the sole resolution source.
The ↑ 2,400 leg showed outcomePrices [1,0], closed true, and umaResolutionStatus resolved in page source.
Kraken showed a same-day ETH/USD high above $2,400, making a Binance threshold breach plausible.
strongest_disconfirmers: I did not independently retrieve the exact Binance historical 1-minute candle that crossed $2,400.
If the page-source resolution state were stale or malformed, confidence would be too high.
persona_runs.timing_relevance: Timing mattered almost entirely through an already-realized threshold-touch catalyst; once that happened, the rest of the week became much less relevant.
persona_runs.source_quality_view: Primary evidence quality is high for contract interpretation but somewhat concentrated on Polymarket page source; independent confirmation is moderate and mainly contextual.

key_assumptions: A Binance 1h high above 2400 implies at least one underlying 1m high above 2400 in that hour.
The observed Binance print falls inside the contract's April 13-19 ET window.
strongest_supports: Polymarket rules explicitly name Binance ETH/USDT 1m candle highs as the settlement source.
Binance ETHUSDT kline data showed a 2415.50 high on 2026-04-14 within the relevant window.
strongest_disconfirmers: The saved verification artifact used Binance 1h candles rather than directly archived 1m candles.
CoinGecko contextual highs were lower, showing non-governing feeds can diverge from Binance.
persona_runs.timing_relevance: High; this is a date-window threshold contract and the key evidence appears to have occurred already on April 14.
persona_runs.source_quality_view: Strong primary rules source plus strong venue-specific verification; residual precision gap from not saving the exact 1m series.

key_assumptions: Binance API minute data matches the operative Binance chart data referenced by Polymarket.
The ET qualifying window was mapped correctly in verification.
No settlement exception invalidates the observed >2400 print.
strongest_supports: Polymarket rules say any Binance ETH/USDT 1-minute candle high >= 2400 in the Apr 13-19 ET window resolves Yes.
Binance verification found a 1-minute high of 2415.5 on 2026-04-14 10:32 ET.
The exceedance is $15.5 above threshold, reducing edge-case rounding risk.
strongest_disconfirmers: Residual risk that Binance API output differs from the exact chart/UI Polymarket expects.
Small possibility of timezone/window interpretation error or settlement dispute.
persona_runs.timing_relevance: High; this is a date-specific threshold market, but the timing risk collapsed once a qualifying high appears to have already printed on Apr 14.
persona_runs.source_quality_view: High quality overall: Polymarket provides the contract wording and Binance provides direct venue data, with low remaining source-of-truth ambiguity after verification.

key_assumptions: The designated Polymarket settlement source probably tracks major spot venue highs closely.
Source-of-truth mismatch risk is real but not dominant.
strongest_supports: Binance ETHUSDT 24h high printed 2415.50, above the $2,400 threshold.
The extreme market price implies traders likely already saw threshold-touch evidence.
strongest_disconfirmers: The exact Polymarket rules and named official data source were not recoverable in-tool, so Binance alone is not fully dispositive.
persona_runs.timing_relevance: This is a short-dated threshold market, so source-of-truth and timing alignment matter more than long-run ETH direction.
persona_runs.source_quality_view: Good direct venue evidence plus useful contract-surface context, but only medium overall confidence because the governing rules text was not directly extracted.

decision_summary.primary_crux: This contract appears effectively already satisfied because bundled Binance ETH/USDT data showed an in-window high of 2415.5 above the 2400 threshold, but the market's 0.916 price no longer offers a clean executable edge once the remaining uncertainty is mostly about settlement-surface parity and archival proof rather than ETH direction.

causal_attribution.note: Evaluator distinguishes observed market/action facts from inferred causal attributions. Attribution population is intentionally left sparse until structured event + market-path reconstruction is implemented.
